stem: Which address prefix range is reserved for IPv4 loopback?
explain_short: 127.0.0.0/8 is reserved for loopback. 224–239 is multicast, 240–254 is experimental, 169.254 is link-local.

explain_lecture.slide: Types of IPv4 Addresses — Special Use IPv4 Addresses
explain_lecture.quote: Loopback addresses 127.0.0.0 /8 (127.0.0.1 to 127.255.255.254) Commonly identified as only 127.0.0.1 Used on a host to test if TCP/IP is operational.

stem: A message is sent to a dedicated host on a remote network. Which type of message is it?
choices: limited broadcast
multicast
directed broadcast
unicast
explain_short: A message to one specific host (dedicated) is unicast.

explain_lecture.slide: IPv4 Unicast, Broadcast, and Multicast — Unicast
explain_lecture.quote: Unicast transmission is sending a packet to one destination IP address.

stem: From the list below, please specify an experimental address.
explain_short: 240.0.0.0/4 is the experimental / reserved Class E range.

explain_lecture.slide: Types of IPv4 Addresses — Legacy Classful Addressing

stem: Which subnet mask would be used if 5 host bits are available?

explain_lecture.quote: Use the formula 2^h - 2 (where h is the number of host bits) to calculate usable host addresses per subnet.

stem: A host is transmitting a multicast. Which host or hosts will receive it?
choices: a specially defined group of hosts
the closest neighbor on the same network
one specific host
directly connected network devices
explain_short: Multicast is delivered to a defined multicast group.

explain_lecture.slide: IPv4 Unicast, Broadcast, and Multicast — Multicast
explain_lecture.quote: Multicast transmission reduces traffic by allowing a host to send a single packet to a selected set of hosts that subscribe to a multicast group.

stem: What is the purpose of the 'ip helper-address' command on a router sub-interface?
choices: To assign a static IP
To exclude IPs from DHCP
To forward DHCP requests to another VLAN
To enable routing between VLANs
explain_short: ip helper-address relays broadcast DHCP requests to a unicast DHCP server.

stem: Which three fields aren't used in a UDP segment header? (Choose three.)
choices: Window Size
Source Port
Acknowledgment Number
Checksum
Sequence Number
explain_short: UDP only has Source Port, Destination Port, Length, and Checksum.

explain_lecture.quote: The UDP header is far simpler than the TCP header because it only has four fields and requires 8 bytes (i.e. 64 bits).

stem: Which two characteristics aren't associated with UDP sessions? (Choose two.)
choices: Destination devices receive traffic with minimal delay.
Transmitted data segments are tracked.
Destination devices reassemble messages and pass them to an application.
Received data is unacknowledged.
explain_short: UDP doesn't track segments and doesn't reassemble in order — those are TCP behaviors.

stem: Which three statements doesn't characterise UDP? (Choose three.)
choices: UDP provides connection-oriented, fast transport of data at Layer 3.
UDP relies on application layer protocols for error detection.
UDP is a low overhead protocol that does not provide sequencing or flow control mechanisms.
UDP relies on IP for error detection and recovery.
UDP provides sophisticated flow control mechanisms.
explain_short: UDP is connectionless (not connection-oriented), works at Layer 4 (not 3), and has no flow control.

stem: In what situation would TCP be better than UDP as the preferred transport layer protocol?
choices: when applications need to guarantee that a packet arrives intact, in sequence, and unduplicated
when a faster delivery mechanism is needed
when delivery overhead is not an issue
when applications do not need to guarantee delivery of the data
when destination port numbers are dynamic
explain_short: TCP guarantees reliable, in-order, unduplicated delivery.

explain_lecture.quote: TCP provides reliable delivery by ensuring that blocks of data sent are delivered, providing flow control and equal sharing of bandwidth.

stem: The ______ protocol is used when one user shares an entire file with another user. A person would load a ______-based application such as WireShark and use that application to locate and access resources shared by others.
choices: Ethernet
Gnutella
POP
SMTP
explain_short: Gnutella is a peer-to-peer file-sharing protocol. (Note: WireShark is a packet analyzer; the original wording is misleading but Gnutella is the intended answer.)

stem: Which applications or services allow hosts to act as client and server at the same time?
choices: client/server applications
email applications
Peer-to-peer applications
authentication services
explain_short: In P2P, every host can act as both client and server.

stem: What is the purpose of the network security authorization function?
choices: to require users to prove who they are
to determine which resources a user can access
to keep track of the actions of a user
to provide challenge and response questions
explain_short: AAA: Authentication = who you are; Authorization = what you can access; Accounting = tracking.

stem: The ______ command is very important to use when log messages appear. Log messages appear by default when a user is directly consoled into a Cisco device, but require the terminal monitor to be entered when a user is accessing a network device remotely.
choices: debug all
logging synchronous
show running-config
terminal monitor
explain_short: terminal monitor sends console messages to the active vty (remote) session.

stem: How many host addresses are available on the 192.168.128.128/27?
explain_short: /27 = 5 host bits → 2^5 - 2 = 30 usable hosts.

explain_lecture.quote: Use the formula 2^h - 2 (where h is the number of host bits) to calculate usable host addresses per subnet.

stem: A site administrator has been told that a particular network at the site must accommodate 30 hosts. Which subnet mask would be used that contains the required number of host bits?
explain_short: 30 hosts needs 5 host bits → /27 → 30 usable.

stem: What route would have the lowest administrative distance?
choices: a directly connected network
a static route
a route received through the EIGRP routing protocol
a route received through the OSPF routing protocol
explain_short: AD: Connected=0, Static=1, EIGRP=90, OSPF=110, RIP=120. Directly connected wins.

explain_lecture.quote: A directly connected route has an administrative distance of 0, the most trustworthy.

stem: Which statements about static routing are correct? (Choose two.)
choices: Static routing is more secure because it does not advertise over the network.
Static routing scales well with expanding networks.
Static routing requires very little knowledge of the network for correct implementation.
Static routing uses fewer router resources than dynamic routing.
Static routing is relatively easy to configure for large networks.
explain_short: Static is secure (no advertisements) and lightweight (less CPU/memory). It scales poorly and requires deep network knowledge.

stem: Which three IOS troubleshooting commands can help to isolate problems with a static route? (Choose three.)
choices: show version
ping
tracert
show ip route
show ip interface brief
show arp
explain_short: ping tests reachability, show ip route confirms the static entry, show ip interface brief checks interfaces. (tracert is the Windows form; IOS uses traceroute.)

stem: An OSPF enabled router is processing learned routes to select best paths to reach a destination network. What is the OSPF algorithm evaluating as the metric?
choices: The amount of traffic and probability of failure of links.
The amount of packet delivery time and slowest bandwidth.
The number of hops along the routing path.
The cumulative bandwidth that is used along the routing path.
explain_short: OSPF cost is based on cumulative bandwidth along the path (cost = reference BW / interface BW).

stem: Which OSPF packet contains the different types of link-state advertisements?
choices: DBD
LSR
hello
LSAck
LSU
explain_short: LSU (Link-State Update) carries one or more LSAs. Flow: Hello → DBD → LSR → LSU → LSAck.

stem: What is used to create the OSPF neighbor table?
choices: adjacency database
link-state database
forwarding database
routing table
explain_short: OSPF neighbor table = adjacency database. LSDB = topology table. Routing table = forwarding info.

stem: What are two functions of a router? (Choose two.)
choices: A router connects multiple IP networks.
It controls the flow of data via the use of Layer 2 addresses.
It determines the best path to send packets.
It provides segmentation at Layer 2.
It builds a routing table based on ARP requests.
explain_short: Routers connect IP networks (L3) and select best paths.

explain_lecture.slide: Path Determination — Functions of a Router
explain_lecture.quote: A router connects multiple networks. This means it has multiple interfaces that each belong to a different IP network.

stem: What is a disadvantage of NAT?
choices: There is no end-to-end addressing.
The router does not need to alter the checksum of the IPv4 packets.
The costs of readdressing hosts can be significant for a publicly addressed network.
The internal hosts have to use a single public IPv4 address for external communication.
explain_short: NAT breaks end-to-end addressing — a key downside, especially for protocols like IPsec.

stem: A network administrator is designing an IPv4 addressing scheme and requires these subnets: 1 subnet of 100 hosts, 2 subnets of 80 hosts, 2 subnets of 30 hosts, 4 subnets of 20 hosts. Which combination of subnets and masks will provide the best addressing plan?
choices: 9 subnets of 126 hosts with a 255.255.255.128 mask
3 subnets of 126 hosts with a 255.255.255.128 mask; 6 subnets of 30 hosts with a 255.255.255.224 mask
3 subnets of 126 hosts with a 255.255.255.192 mask; 6 subnets of 30 hosts with a 255.255.255.240 mask
1 subnet of 126 hosts with a 255.255.255.192 mask; 2 subnets of 80 hosts with a 255.255.255.224 mask; 6 subnets of 30 hosts with a 255.255.255.240 mask
explain_short: 100- and 80-host nets need /25 (126 hosts). 30- and 20-host nets fit /27 (30 hosts). Total: 3 × /25 + 6 × /27.

stem: What benefit does NAT64 provide?
choices: It allows sites to use private IPv6 addresses and translates them to global IPv6 addresses.
It allows sites to connect multiple IPv4 hosts to the Internet via the use of a single public IPv4 address.
It allows sites to connect IPv6 hosts to an IPv4 network by translating the IPv6 addresses to IPv4 addresses.
It allows sites to use private IPv4 addresses, and thus hides the internal addressing structure from hosts on public IPv4 networks.
explain_short: NAT64 translates between IPv6 and IPv4 to allow IPv6-only hosts to reach IPv4 networks.

stem: A router needs to be configured to route within OSPF area 0. Which two commands are required to accomplish this? (Choose two.)
choices: RouterA(config)# router ospf 0
RouterA(config)# router ospf 1
RouterA(config-router)# network 192.168.2.0 0.0.0.255 0
RouterA(config-router)# network 192.168.2.0 0.0.0.255 area 0
RouterA(config-router)# network 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 0
explain_short: Process ID must be 1–65535 (not 0). Network statement requires wildcard mask and 'area' keyword.

stem: What is a socket?
choices: the combination of the source and destination IP address and source and destination Ethernet address
the combination of a source IP address and port number, or a destination IP address and port number
the combination of the source and destination sequence and acknowledgment numbers
the combination of the source and destination sequence numbers and port numbers
explain_short: A socket = IP address + port number.

explain_lecture.quote: The combination of the source IP address and source port number, or the destination IP address and destination port number is known as a socket.

stem: A client creates a packet to send to a server. The client is requesting SSH service. What number will be used as the destination port number in the sending packet?
explain_short: SSH = TCP/22. (Telnet=23, HTTP=80, TFTP=69, DHCP=67/68.)

stem: What is the difference between a virus and a worm?
choices: Viruses self-replicate but worms do not.
Worms self-replicate but viruses do not.
Worms require a host file but viruses do not.
Viruses hide in legitimate programs but worms do not.
explain_short: Worms self-replicate across networks without user action. Viruses need a host file/user execution.

stem: Which addresses are valid private addresses? (Choose all that apply.)
Which statement is true about variable-length subnet masking?
choices: Each subnet is the same size.
The size of each subnet may be different, depending on requirements.
Subnets may only be subnetted one additional time.
Bits are returned, rather than borrowed, to create additional subnets.
explain_short: VLSM allows different mask lengths per subnet to fit varying host counts.

stem: Which scenario describes a function provided by the transport layer?
choices: A student is using a classroom VoIP phone to call home. The unique identifier burned into the phone is a transport layer address used to contact another network device on the same network.
A student is playing a short web-based movie with sound. The movie and sound are encoded within the transport layer header.
A student has two web browser windows open in order to access two web sites. The transport layer ensures the correct web page is delivered to the correct browser window.

A corporate worker is accessing a web server located on a corporate network. The transport layer formats the screen so the web page appears properly no matter what device is being used to view the web site.
explain_short: Multiplexing/demultiplexing via port numbers is a transport-layer function.

stem: Which example of malicious code would be classified as a worm?
choices: malware that was written to look like a video game
malware that requires manual user intervention to spread between systems
malware that attaches itself to a legitimate program and spreads to other programs when launched
malware that can automatically spread from one system to another by exploiting a vulnerability in the target
explain_short: Worms self-propagate by exploiting vulnerabilities — no user action needed.

stem: How does FCS help a network support applications and services?
choices: by limiting the impact of a network failure
by allowing quick recovery from network failures
by providing mechanisms to manage congested network traffic
by providing the ability for the network to grow to accommodate new users
explain_short: FCS here refers to fault-containment / fault tolerance in the Cisco curriculum context. (FCS as 'Frame Check Sequence' is unrelated.)

stem: Which two protocols operate at the lowest layer of the TCP/IP protocol stack? (Choose two.)
choices: DNS
Ethernet
IP
POP
TCP
UDP
explain_short: The lowest layer of TCP/IP is Network Access (Link). Ethernet is the only Network Access protocol in this list. IP operates at Internet layer (one above).

stem: A dedicated server is needed when implementing a peer-to-peer network.
choices: true
false
explain_short: P2P does not require a dedicated server — that's the whole point.

choices: Site 1
Site 2
Site 3
Site 4
explain_short: Site 2 has 70 hosts assigned to 192.168.2.64/25. /25 boundaries within a /24 must start at .0 or .128. So 192.168.2.64/25 is an invalid network address — should be 192.168.2.0/25 or 192.168.2.128/25.

stem: Static routes are configured by the use of the ______ global configuration command.
explain_short: Syntax: ip route [destination_network] [mask] [next-hop | exit-interface]

stem: When a router receives a packet, it examines the destination address of the packet and looks in the ______ to determine the best path to use to forward the packet.
explain_short: The routing table holds the best paths the router has learned. The router consults it on every forwarded packet.
